title: Job Openings at Smile 360 Dental Specialists
content:
(adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    

Smile 360 Dental Specialists (ODE) is a leading supplier of products and appliances in the dental supply chain industry, committed to providing high-quality products and exceptional service to dental professionals. We strive to innovate and enhance the dental industry through our comprehensive product range and customer-centric approach. We are the leading dental clinic in Nigeria based on our customer’s satisfaction and awards won. Amongst many others, we are the “Winner of the Best Dental Service Provider of the year 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019 and 2021 consecutively, by the Nigerian Healthcare Excellence Award”. We are also the first and only clinic with Orthodontics, Dento-facial Orthopaedics, and pediatric dentistry. Our other services include Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery, Implant Dentistry Endodontic, Family dentistry, and Periodontics.

Our practice is extremely customer-friendly, ensuring that every visit is stress-free, memorable, fun, and worthwhile. We are passionate about oral health, giving superior customer interaction, and first-class standard services that you would expect in London or Dubai. With proficient and tested professionals committed to making every visit to our world-class luxurious facility seamless and enjoyable at every level.

We are recruiting to fill the following positions below:

1.) Dentist

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are looking for an experienced and skilled Dentist with at least 2years of experience to join our team at Smile360.
	The ideal candidate will have a proven track record of providing high-quality dental care and possess the expertise to handle a wide range of dental procedures.


Responsibilities


	Perform comprehensive dental examinations and assessments
	Create tailored treatment plans and provide exceptional dental care to patients
	Administer various dental procedures including restorative, cosmetic, surgical, and emergency treatments
	Educate patients on oral health maintenance and preventive care
	Collaborate with a multidisciplinary team to ensure optimal patient outcomes
	Maintain accurate and detailed patient records in compliance with regulations
	Stay current with advancements in dentistry and participate in professional development activities


Requirements


	Degree in Dental Medicine or Surgery from an accredited Institution
	A minimum of 2 years of experience practicing as a Dentist in a clinical setting
	Valid dental license
	Proficiency in performing a wide range of dental procedures with precision and efficiency
	Excellent communication skills and ability to build rapport with patients
	Strong decision-making and problem-solving abilities
	Commitment to delivering compassionate and personalized care to every patient
	Willingness to mentor and supervise junior dental staff as needed.


Benefits


	Competitive salary and performance-based incentives.
	Health insurance.
	Pension
	Leave.


Application Closing Date: 30th March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their resume and a cover letter outlining their qualifications to:  [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

 

 

2.) Chief Operating Officer (COO) – Enterprise Integrator

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Summary


	This is not a traditional clinic COO role.
	The organisation is seeking an Enterprise Integrator — a senior operational leader capable of translating strategic priorities into disciplined execution across multiple business functions.
	The COO will play a central role in strengthening operational systems, improving margin performance, building scalable governance frameworks, and ensuring alignment across leadership teams.


Core Responsibilities
Enterprise Operational Leadership:


	 Translate organisational strategy into measurable operational execution
	Strengthen cross-functional coordination and execution discipline
	Improve organisational operating rhythms and decision-making speed
	Drive operational performance management across departments.


Financial Performance & Margin Discipline:


	Drive EBITDA and margin improvement through operational discipline
	Identify and eliminate margin leakage across the organisation
	Strengthen cost governance, procurement discipline, and working capital management
	Support financial sustainability through operational efficiency.


Revenue Performance & Conversion Optimisation:


	Improve conversion performance and revenue realisation
	Align marketing, clinical capacity, and patient experience to support growth
	Strengthen operational drivers of revenue and service utilisation.


Systems, Processes & Governance:


	Institutionalise SOPs, operational dashboards, and performance frameworks
	Strengthen governance, reporting, and compliance structures
	Build scalable operational systems that support enterprise growth
	Improve operational visibility and leadership accountability.


Leadership & Organisational Effectiveness:


	Provide execution leadership across multiple business units
	Strengthen accountability and performance management across departments
	Build a disciplined, ownership-driven leadership culture
	Reduce executive firefighting by implementing effective systems.


Success Metrics (First 12–18 Months)


	EBITDA and margin improvement
	Improved conversion and revenue realisation
	Stronger operational governance and systems discipline
	Improved leadership accountability and execution speed
	Operational readiness for continued organisational growth.


Ideal Candidate Profile:


	We are seeking a senior operator with proven experience scaling complex service organisations.


Required Experience:


	12–15 years of progressive leadership experience
	Proven senior operator in multi-site healthcare environments (hospital, diagnostics, dental, or similar)
	Demonstrated success in improving margin performance and operational efficiency
	Track record of building systems and scaling enterprise operations
	Experience operating within fast-growing or founder-led organisations.


Key Capabilities:
The successful candidate must demonstrate:


	Strong financial acumen with margin and cost optimisation impact
	Ability to translate strategy into disciplined execution
	Strong leadership presence and ability to influence senior teams
	High execution capacity, emotional intelligence, and governance mindset
	Ability to lead senior teams and influence strong personalities
	Comfort operating as an execution partner to a founder-CEO.


Reporting Structure:


	The Chief Operating Officer will report directly to the Chief Executive Officer and serve as a core member of the Executive Leadership Team.
	This is a Board-visible leadership role responsible for driving operational execution and strengthening organisational performance.


Why Join Smile360
This role offers a unique opportunity to:


	Partner directly with the CEO in shaping the next phase of organisational growth
	Build scalable operational systems in a fast-growing healthcare platform
	Drive measurable financial and operational impact
	Lead enterprise execution across a growing multi-location healthcare organisation
	This is a role for a builder and operator — someone passionate about improving systems, strengthening governance, and driving disciplined execution.


Application Closing Date: 31st March, 2026.

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV and executive profile to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the mail.

 

 

3.) Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a passionate and customer-centric individual to join our team as a Customer Experience Officer.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring an outstanding experience for our patients from the moment they enter our practice until their departure.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome patients upon arrival, providing a warm and friendly atmosphere
	Manage patient appointments, inquiries, and follow-ups promptly and efficiently
	Ensure the waiting area and reception are clean, organized, and presentable
	Assist in maintaining patient records and confidential information
	Coordinate with the clinical team to ensure smooth patient flow and address any patient concerns
	Collect feedback from patients to continuously improve our services and patient experience
	Handle payment transactions and insurance claims accurately.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Hospitality, or related field
	Proven experience in a customer service role, preferably in a healthcare setting
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Knowledge of dental procedures and terminology is a plus
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Training and professional development opportunities
	Health insurance coverage
	Friendly and supportive work environment
	Opportunity to grow within a reputable dental practice.


Application Closing Date: 31st March, 2026.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter outlining their qualifications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Field Mechanical Engineer",
  "description": "JMG is a diversified solution provider with a broad portfolio in power generation, electrical infrastructures, and industrial equipment. With over 20 years of experience in Africa and strong partnerships with some of the world&rsquo;s leading brands, JMG combines global technologies and regional expertise to fulfill the African market&#39;s development needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Mechanical Engineer

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly skilled and self-motivated&nbsp;Field Mechanical Engineer&nbsp;to join our Aftersales team.
	In this role, you will be responsible for the troubleshooting, maintenance, repair, and overhaul of diesel and gas generator sets at customer sites.
	You will ensure minimal downtime and maximum efficiency for our clients&#39; critical power systems.
	You must be comfortable working independently, managing a busy service schedule, and diagnosing complex mechanical issues in the field.


Minimum Requirements


	Perform routine preventative maintenance on generator sets (oil changes, filter replacements, coolant checks) according to OEM specifications and service schedules.
	Diagnose and repair mechanical faults on diesel engines (e.g., Perkins, Cummins, Deutz, MTU, Caterpillar), alternators, and cooling systems.
	Conduct load bank testing, vibration analysis, and thermographic surveys to ensure system health.
	Overhaul engines and components (cylinder heads, fuel injectors, turbochargers) as required.
	Respond to emergency breakdown calls and provide rapid on-site troubleshooting to restore power.
	Analyze control system alarms and mechanical symptoms to identify root causes of failure.
	Provide technical guidance to clients on generator operation and minor troubleshooting.
	Conduct site surveys to assess installation conditions, fuel quality, and exhaust systems.
	Prepare detailed and accurate service reports, timesheets, and condition reports immediately after each site visit.
	Advise clients on potential issues, recommended repairs, and future service requirements.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mechanical Engineering, Marine Engineering, or a related field.
	Must have 5 &ndash; 7 years of hands-on experience in servicing and repairing diesel/gas generators (10 kVA &ndash; 1000 kVA+).
	Deep understanding of 4-stroke diesel engine principles, alternator construction (AVR function), fuel systems, and cooling systems.
	Valid driver&rsquo;s license (ability to drive a manual vehicle is essential).
	Proficiency with mechanical diagnostic tools, digital multimeters, and engine control unit (ECU) software is highly desirable.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;career@jmglimited.com&nbsp;using the job title subject of the mail. OR

Field Mechanical Engineer Job at JMG




        
    

        
        JMG is a diversified solution provider with a broad portfolio in power generation, electrical infrastructures, and industrial equipment. With over 20 years of experience in Africa and strong partnerships with some of the world’s leading brands, JMG combines global technologies and regional expertise to fulfill the African market's development needs.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Field Mechanical Engineer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly skilled and self-motivated Field Mechanical Engineer to join our Aftersales team.
	In this role, you will be responsible for the troubleshooting, maintenance, repair, and overhaul of diesel and gas generator sets at customer sites.
	You will ensure minimal downtime and maximum efficiency for our clients' critical power systems.
	You must be comfortable working independently, managing a busy service schedule, and diagnosing complex mechanical issues in the field.


Minimum Requirements


	Perform routine preventative maintenance on generator sets (oil changes, filter replacements, coolant checks) according to OEM specifications and service schedules.
	Diagnose and repair mechanical faults on diesel engines (e.g., Perkins, Cummins, Deutz, MTU, Caterpillar), alternators, and cooling systems.
	Conduct load bank testing, vibration analysis, and thermographic surveys to ensure system health.
	Overhaul engines and components (cylinder heads, fuel injectors, turbochargers) as required.
	Respond to emergency breakdown calls and provide rapid on-site troubleshooting to restore power.
	Analyze control system alarms and mechanical symptoms to identify root causes of failure.
	Provide technical guidance to clients on generator operation and minor troubleshooting.
	Conduct site surveys to assess installation conditions, fuel quality, and exhaust systems.
	Prepare detailed and accurate service reports, timesheets, and condition reports immediately after each site visit.
	Advise clients on potential issues, recommended repairs, and future service requirements.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Mechanical Engineering, Marine Engineering, or a related field.
	Must have 5 – 7 years of hands-on experience in servicing and repairing diesel/gas generators (10 kVA – 1000 kVA+).
	Deep understanding of 4-stroke diesel engine principles, alternator construction (AVR function), fuel systems, and cooling systems.
	Valid driver’s license (ability to drive a manual vehicle is essential).
	Proficiency with mechanical diagnostic tools, digital multimeters, and engine control unit (ECU) software is highly desirable.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the job title subject of the mail. OR

 Click here to Apply Online    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Associate",
  "description": "Daystar Power is a leading off-grid power service provider, offering hybrid power solutions to commercial and industrial businesses in West Africa. Daystar Power&rsquo;s solutions &ldquo;Solar-as-a-Service&rdquo; (100% solar power) and &ldquo;Power-as-a-Service&rdquo; (hybrid power solutions with battery storage) provide clean and reliable power while significantly reducing clients&rsquo; overall power costs.

Daystar Power counts the region&rsquo;s leading industrial and commercial companies among its client base. Daystar is active in Nigeria, Ghana, Togo and Senegal with a representative office in Cote d&rsquo;Ivoire.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Associate
Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos
Schedule: Full-time

Description


	We are looking to engage a competent and experienced HR Associate in Lagos, Nigeria, who will provide general HR support to the group&rsquo;s businesses.
	We are seeking hard working, motivated, and well-organized individuals with a focus on continuous improvement to exceed expectations.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment of high-performance teams:


	Facilitate the recruitment process commencing from the selection of high-performance candidates, interviewing, due diligence, preparation of employment contracts and employee induction and training.
	Maintenance of up-to-date job requirements for the various departments and job descriptions for vacancies.
	Manage recruiting and human resources budgets.


Performance Management:


	Draft and manage key performance indicators for employees.
	Planning, monitoring, and appraisal of employee output.
	Oversee and manage performance appraisal system that drives high performance.


Human Resources Analytics:


	Manage, track and draw insightful data-based inferences from Employee data on retention, recruitments, diversity, equity, inclusion and other relevant ratios.
	Design and manage interactive HR dashboards from various Employee metrics for visualization and automations.
	Prepare required reports, present findings and recommend Employee Engagement initiatives.


Human Resources Operations and Statutory Compliance:


	Oversee the functional HR budget and manage expenses within budget allocations.
	Maintain and update relevant databases (recruitment, performance history, employee database, organisation structures, training, etc)
	Draft and update human resource policies and procedures.
	Provide consultation to management on employee relations issues and effectively manage disciplinary issues within the company.
	Process documentation and prepare reports relating to personnel activities (staffing, recruitment, training, grievances, performance evaluations etc.)
	Coordinate and document HR projects (meetings, training, surveys etc.)
	Deal with employee requests regarding human resources issues, rules, and regulations.
	Support current and future business needs through the development, engagement, motivation and preservation of human capital.


Requirements


	Education: Relevant degree with excellent grades and HR certification.
	Experience: 3-5 years of HR operations/admin experience within a fast-paced structured business environment. Strong preference will be given to applicants that have worked in a multinational corporation.
	Language Proficiency:&nbsp;English (excellent oral &amp; writing skills).


Knowledge and skills:


	Proven experience and knowledge of Nigerian labour law and regulations.
	Knowledge of HR Systems &amp; Processes i.e. HR Policies, Recruitment, Selection &amp; Deployment, Performance Management, Career Development, Training Management, Compensation Administration, Employee Communication, Workforce Profiling, Employee Data Management.
	Excellent knowledge of Microsoft office applications in particular, MS Word, Excel , PowerPoint and Microsoft Dynamics 365.
	Excellent written and verbal communications skills and the ability to establish and maintain strong relationships.
	Basic Project Management.
	Organisation/Administration skills.
	Ability to work independently with little or no supervision.
	Ability to critically analyse complex problems and provide structured solutions.
	Comfortable with working as part of a team.
	Willingness to work flexible hours if necessary.
	Possess keen eyes for details.
	Creativity &amp; Innovation.


Benefits


	Opportunity to work in a forward-looking, innovative company, with international group of colleagues.
	Unlimited career opportunities - depending on achievements and personal development.
	A competitive salary and benefits package with long term prospect in a fast-growing company.


Note


	Daystar Power Group is an equal opportunity employer committed to promoting diversity and inclusion in the workplace.
	We prohibit all forms of discrimination and harassment based on race, colour, gender, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local law.
	Qualified women are encouraged to apply.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.

HR Associate Job at Daystar Power Group




        
    

        
        Daystar Power is a leading off-grid power service provider, offering hybrid power solutions to commercial and industrial businesses in West Africa. Daystar Power’s solutions “Solar-as-a-Service” (100% solar power) and “Power-as-a-Service” (hybrid power solutions with battery storage) provide clean and reliable power while significantly reducing clients’ overall power costs.

Daystar Power counts the region’s leading industrial and commercial companies among its client base. Daystar is active in Nigeria, Ghana, Togo and Senegal with a representative office in Cote d’Ivoire.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Job Position: HR Associate
Job Location: Lagos
Schedule: Full-time

Description


	We are looking to engage a competent and experienced HR Associate in Lagos, Nigeria, who will provide general HR support to the group’s businesses.
	We are seeking hard working, motivated, and well-organized individuals with a focus on continuous improvement to exceed expectations.


Key Responsibilities
Recruitment of high-performance teams:


	Facilitate the recruitment process commencing from the selection of high-performance candidates, interviewing, due diligence, preparation of employment contracts and employee induction and training.
	Maintenance of up-to-date job requirements for the various departments and job descriptions for vacancies.
	Manage recruiting and human resources budgets.


Performance Management:


	Draft and manage key performance indicators for employees.
	Planning, monitoring, and appraisal of employee output.
	Oversee and manage performance appraisal system that drives high performance.


Human Resources Analytics:


	Manage, track and draw insightful data-based inferences from Employee data on retention, recruitments, diversity, equity, inclusion and other relevant ratios.
	Design and manage interactive HR dashboards from various Employee metrics for visualization and automations.
	Prepare required reports, present findings and recommend Employee Engagement initiatives.


Human Resources Operations and Statutory Compliance:


	Oversee the functional HR budget and manage expenses within budget allocations.
	Maintain and update relevant databases (recruitment, performance history, employee database, organisation structures, training, etc)
	Draft and update human resource policies and procedures.
	Provide consultation to management on employee relations issues and effectively manage disciplinary issues within the company.
	Process documentation and prepare reports relating to personnel activities (staffing, recruitment, training, grievances, performance evaluations etc.)
	Coordinate and document HR projects (meetings, training, surveys etc.)
	Deal with employee requests regarding human resources issues, rules, and regulations.
	Support current and future business needs through the development, engagement, motivation and preservation of human capital.


Requirements


	Education: Relevant degree with excellent grades and HR certification.
	Experience: 3-5 years of HR operations/admin experience within a fast-paced structured business environment. Strong preference will be given to applicants that have worked in a multinational corporation.
	Language Proficiency: English (excellent oral & writing skills).


Knowledge and skills:


	Proven experience and knowledge of Nigerian labour law and regulations.
	Knowledge of HR Systems & Processes i.e. HR Policies, Recruitment, Selection & Deployment, Performance Management, Career Development, Training Management, Compensation Administration, Employee Communication, Workforce Profiling, Employee Data Management.
	Excellent knowledge of Microsoft office applications in particular, MS Word, Excel , PowerPoint and Microsoft Dynamics 365.
	Excellent written and verbal communications skills and the ability to establish and maintain strong relationships.
	Basic Project Management.
	Organisation/Administration skills.
	Ability to work independently with little or no supervision.
	Ability to critically analyse complex problems and provide structured solutions.
	Comfortable with working as part of a team.
	Willingness to work flexible hours if necessary.
	Possess keen eyes for details.
	Creativity & Innovation.


Benefits


	Opportunity to work in a forward-looking, innovative company, with international group of colleagues.
	Unlimited career opportunities - depending on achievements and personal development.
	A competitive salary and benefits package with long term prospect in a fast-growing company.


Note


	Daystar Power Group is an equal opportunity employer committed to promoting diversity and inclusion in the workplace.
	We prohibit all forms of discrimination and harassment based on race, colour, gender, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local law.
	Qualified women are encouraged to apply.
	Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Customer Service Officer",
  "description": "Smile 360 Dental Specialists -&nbsp;We are the leading dental clinic in Nigeria based on our customer&rsquo;s satisfaction and awards won. Amongst many others, we are the &ldquo;Winner of the Best Dental Service Provider of the year 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019 and 2021 consecutively, by the Nigerian Healthcare Excellence Award&rdquo;.&nbsp;We are also the first and only clinic with Orthodontics, Dento-facial Orthopaedics, and pediatric dentistry. Our other services include Oral &amp; Maxillofacial Surgery, Implant Dentistry Endodontic, Family dentistry, and Periodontics.

Our practice is extremely customer-friendly, ensuring that every visit is stress-free, memorable, fun, and worthwhile. We are passionate about oral health, giving superior customer interaction, and first-class standard services that you would expect in London or Dubai. With proficient and tested professionals committed to making every visit to our world-class luxurious facility seamless and enjoyable at every level.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a passionate and customer-centric individual to join our team as a Customer Experience Officer.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring an outstanding experience for our patients from the moment they enter our practice until their departure.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome patients upon arrival, providing a warm and friendly atmosphere
	Manage patient appointments, inquiries, and follow-ups promptly and efficiently
	Ensure the waiting area and reception are clean, organized, and presentable
	Assist in maintaining patient records and confidential information
	Coordinate with the clinical team to ensure smooth patient flow and address any patient concerns
	Collect feedback from patients to continuously improve our services and patient experience
	Handle payment transactions and insurance claims accurately.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in Business Administration, Hospitality, or related field
	Proven experience in a customer service role, preferably in a healthcare setting
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Knowledge of dental procedures and terminology is a plus
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Training and professional development opportunities
	Health insurance coverage
	Friendly and supportive work environment
	Opportunity to grow within a reputable dental practice.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter outlining their qualifications to:&nbsp;jobs@smile360ng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Customer Service Officer Job at Smile 360 Dental Specialists




        
    

        
        Smile 360 Dental Specialists - We are the leading dental clinic in Nigeria based on our customer’s satisfaction and awards won. Amongst many others, we are the “Winner of the Best Dental Service Provider of the year 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019 and 2021 consecutively, by the Nigerian Healthcare Excellence Award”. We are also the first and only clinic with Orthodontics, Dento-facial Orthopaedics, and pediatric dentistry. Our other services include Oral & Maxillofacial Surgery, Implant Dentistry Endodontic, Family dentistry, and Periodontics.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



Our practice is extremely customer-friendly, ensuring that every visit is stress-free, memorable, fun, and worthwhile. We are passionate about oral health, giving superior customer interaction, and first-class standard services that you would expect in London or Dubai. With proficient and tested professionals committed to making every visit to our world-class luxurious facility seamless and enjoyable at every level.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Customer Service Officer

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Description


	We are seeking a passionate and customer-centric individual to join our team as a Customer Experience Officer.
	The ideal candidate will be responsible for ensuring an outstanding experience for our patients from the moment they enter our practice until their departure.


Key Responsibilities


	Greet and welcome patients upon arrival, providing a warm and friendly atmosphere
	Manage patient appointments, inquiries, and follow-ups promptly and efficiently
	Ensure the waiting area and reception are clean, organized, and presentable
	Assist in maintaining patient records and confidential information
	Coordinate with the clinical team to ensure smooth patient flow and address any patient concerns
	Collect feedback from patients to continuously improve our services and patient experience
	Handle payment transactions and insurance claims accurately.


Qualifications


	Bachelor's degree in Business Administration, Hospitality, or related field
	Proven experience in a customer service role, preferably in a healthcare setting
	Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
	Strong organizational and multitasking abilities
	Knowledge of dental procedures and terminology is a plus
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications.


Benefits


	Competitive salary
	Training and professional development opportunities
	Health insurance coverage
	Friendly and supportive work environment
	Opportunity to grow within a reputable dental practice.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should submit their resume and a cover letter outlining their qualifications to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Process Engineer - Engineering Design",
  "description": "Sterling Oil Exploration and Energy Production Company (SEEPCO) is&nbsp;an Oil Exploration &amp; Energy Production Company Ltd. We are continuously striving to innovate, grow and impact communities around us.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Process Engineer - Engineering Design

Job Location:&nbsp;Lagos

Responsibilities


	Monitor and optimize plant performance for Electrolysis, Brine Treatment, Chlorine Handling, Hydrogen, and HCl systems.
	Review and validate PFD, P&amp;IDs, Heat &amp; Material Balance.
	Conduct process simulations and performance analysis.
	Ensure compliance with environmental, safety, and statutory regulations.
	Participate in HAZOP, Risk Assessments, and Process Safety Reviews.
	Troubleshoot process upsets and implement corrective actions.
	Monitor product quality (Caustic, Chlorine, HCl) and yield optimization.
	Coordinate with Mechanical, Electrical, and Instrumentation teams.
	Support commissioning, start-up, and performance guarantee tests.
	Review vendor technical documents and process guarantees.
	Implement process improvement and energy optimization initiatives.
	Prepare SOPs, operating manuals, and technical reports.
	Ensure safe handling of hazardous chemicals (Cl?, H?, HCl, NaOH).


Requirements
Skills:


	Strong knowledge of Membrane Cell Technology / Electrolysis Process.
	Expertise in Brine purification and dechlorination systems.
	Process Calculation and Engineering Design
	Understanding of Chlorine compression, drying, liquefaction systems.
	Knowledge of Hydrogen handling and safety systems.
	Familiarity with Process simulation tools (Aspen HYSYS / Aspen Plus).
	Experience in Mass &amp; Energy Balance calculations.
	Understanding of Corrosion and material selection for chlorine service.
	Knowledge of Process Safety Management (PSM).
	Strong troubleshooting and root cause analysis skills.
	Familiarity with ISO, IEC, API, and environmental standards.
	Experience in Effluent Treatment and environmental compliance.


Method of Application&nbsp;

Process Engineer - Engineering Design Job at Sterling Oil Exploration & Energy Production Company (SEEPCO)




        
    

        
        Sterling Oil Exploration and Energy Production Company (SEEPCO) is an Oil Exploration & Energy Production Company Ltd. We are continuously striving to innovate, grow and impact communities around us.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Process Engineer - Engineering Design

Job Location: Lagos

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Monitor and optimize plant performance for Electrolysis, Brine Treatment, Chlorine Handling, Hydrogen, and HCl systems.
	Review and validate PFD, P&IDs, Heat & Material Balance.
	Conduct process simulations and performance analysis.
	Ensure compliance with environmental, safety, and statutory regulations.
	Participate in HAZOP, Risk Assessments, and Process Safety Reviews.
	Troubleshoot process upsets and implement corrective actions.
	Monitor product quality (Caustic, Chlorine, HCl) and yield optimization.
	Coordinate with Mechanical, Electrical, and Instrumentation teams.
	Support commissioning, start-up, and performance guarantee tests.
	Review vendor technical documents and process guarantees.
	Implement process improvement and energy optimization initiatives.
	Prepare SOPs, operating manuals, and technical reports.
	Ensure safe handling of hazardous chemicals (Cl?, H?, HCl, NaOH).


Requirements
Skills:


	Strong knowledge of Membrane Cell Technology / Electrolysis Process.
	Expertise in Brine purification and dechlorination systems.
	Process Calculation and Engineering Design
	Understanding of Chlorine compression, drying, liquefaction systems.
	Knowledge of Hydrogen handling and safety systems.
	Familiarity with Process simulation tools (Aspen HYSYS / Aspen Plus).
	Experience in Mass & Energy Balance calculations.
	Understanding of Corrosion and material selection for chlorine service.
	Knowledge of Process Safety Management (PSM).
	Strong troubleshooting and root cause analysis skills.
	Familiarity with ISO, IEC, API, and environmental standards.
	Experience in Effluent Treatment and environmental compliance.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.
